items.description: WezTerm smart paste becomes a route-only dispatcher and no longer owns clipboard decoding, watcher state, or image staging for the standard flow.
steps: Open a direct WezTerm WSL pane, a direct Neovim pane, and a direct PI pane.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V in each pane with representative clipboard contents.
Verify the active in-pane owner handles the paste instead of WezTerm materializing clipboard payloads itself.

items.description: Direct zsh shell smart paste handles both text and image clipboard payloads outside tmux using a single authoritative shell-owned implementation.
steps: Open a direct zsh shell in WezTerm outside tmux.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with text clipboard data and verify the text appears in the shell input buffer.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with image clipboard data and verify a usable Linux path is inserted into the shell input buffer.

items.description: tmux zsh shell smart paste preserves the same shell-owned text and image behavior through minimal tmux metadata passthrough.
steps: Open zsh inside tmux in WezTerm.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with text clipboard data and verify the text appears in the shell input buffer.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with image clipboard data and verify a usable Linux path is inserted into the shell input buffer.

items.description: Direct Neovim smart paste remains application-owned and reliably inserts text or image-path references outside tmux.
steps: Open Neovim directly in WezTerm outside tmux.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with text clipboard data and verify the text is inserted into the current buffer.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with image clipboard data and verify an image path reference is inserted into the current buffer.

items.description: tmux Neovim smart paste remains application-owned and reliably inserts text or image-path references through tmux passthrough.
steps: Open Neovim inside tmux in WezTerm.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with text clipboard data and verify the text is inserted into the current buffer.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with image clipboard data and verify an image path reference is inserted into the current buffer.

items.description: Direct PI smart paste remains application-owned and reliably inserts text or @path payloads outside tmux.
steps: Open PI directly in WezTerm outside tmux.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with text clipboard data and verify the text is inserted into the PI editor.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with image clipboard data and verify an @path payload is inserted into the PI editor.

items.description: tmux PI smart paste remains application-owned and reliably inserts text or @path payloads through tmux passthrough.
steps: Open PI inside tmux in WezTerm.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with text clipboard data and verify the text is inserted into the PI editor.
Press Ctrl+Shift+V with image clipboard data and verify an @path payload is inserted into the PI editor.

items.description: A repo-tracked deterministic E2E verification harness proves the real 12-case smart-paste matrix against the Windows WezTerm + WSL environment.
steps: Seed Windows clipboard text and image payloads from the verification environment.
Run the repo-tracked smart-paste E2E harness against direct shell, direct Neovim, direct PI, tmux shell, tmux Neovim, and tmux PI.
Verify the harness exits 0 and records proof for all 12 text/image cases without bypass.
